title: Linear Regulated Power Supply PCB
connections: AC Input → Bridge Rectifier (4x 1N4007 Diodes)
Bridge Rectifier Output (+) → 1000µF Filter Cap (+) → LM7805 Vin
LM7805 Vout → 100nF Decoupling Cap (+) → DC Output Terminal (+)
LM7805 GND & Cap Negative leads → Common Ground Plane
code:
Layout & Design Steps:
1. Schematic Capture: Draw AC bridge rectifier, filter capacitor, regulator, and output caps.
2. Thermal Management: Place LM7805 with a dedicated tab footprint to mount a heatsink.
3. Trace Width Calculation: Set AC input and unregulated DC traces to at least 1.5mm (60 mils) to support 1.5A load currents.
4. Grounding: Connect negative pins via a solid, low-impedance ground pour.
5. DRC Check: Ensure clearances are at least 0.254mm (10 mils).
tips: Place the large electrolytic capacitor as close to the input pins of the LM7805 regulator as possible to reduce voltage ripple.
The LM7805 regulator dissipates heat equal to (Vin - Vout) × Iload. Always mount a heatsink if voltage drop is higher than 5V at high load.

title: Sensor Breakout PCB (I2C Module)
connections: BME280 VDD → 3.3V Pin
BME280 GND → GND Plane
BME280 SDA → SDA Pin (+ 10kΩ pull-up to 3.3V)
BME280 SCL → SCL Pin (+ 10kΩ pull-up to 3.3V)
code:
Layout & Design Steps:
1. Footprint Selection: Use standard LGA-8 footprint for BME280 chip (requires precision soldering pads).
2. Decoupling: Place the 100nF capacitor directly adjacent to the VDD pin (distance < 2mm).
3. I2C Traces: Route SDA and SCL parallel to keep capacitance matched, avoiding close runs to power traces.
4. Copper Pour: Keep a ground pour on both the top and bottom layers, linked by stitching vias.
5. Output Headers: Place a 2.54mm pitch pin header for breadboard integration.
tips: Do not route digital signals directly under the BME280 sensor cavity to prevent clock noise coupling into sensitive pressure sensors.
I2C pull-up resistors are mandatory — 4.7kΩ or 10kΩ resistors pull up open-drain SDA/SCL lines.

title: Optoisolated Relay Driver Board
connections: Input Signal → 220Ω Resistor → PC817 Opto LED Anode
PC817 Photo-Transistor Collector → 5V Supply, Emitter → BC847 Base
BC847 Collector → Relay Coil (-), Emitter → GND
1N4148 Diode Cathode → Relay Coil (+), Anode → Relay Coil (-)
code:
Layout & Design Steps:
1. Electrical Isolation: Maintain a physical isolation gap of at least 8mm under the optocoupler between low-voltage digital circuits and high-voltage relay rails.
2. Trace Width: Scale AC trace tracks to 2.5mm (100 mils) to support up to 5A load limits.
3. Creepage distances: Mill slots into the FR4 board directly under the relay contacts to prevent high-voltage surface arcing.
4. Snubber Circuit: Place the freewheeling diode directly parallel to the relay coil leads.
tips: Optoisolation blocks high-voltage inductive switching noise from jumping back into sensitive microcontroller GPIO lines.
Always design screw terminal traces without solder mask (exposed copper) so they can be reinforced with solder to support high currents.

title: Synchronous Buck Converter PCB
connections: Vin Terminal → Input Electrolytic Cap (+) → LM2596 Vin
LM2596 OUT → Schottky Diode Cathode & Inductor Input
Inductor Output → Output Filter Cap (+) → Vout Terminal (+)
Feedback Divider resistors → LM2596 Feedback Pin
code:
Layout & Design Steps:
1. Loop Minimization: Group the input capacitor, LM2596 OUT pin, Schottky diode, and inductor closely to minimize high-frequency switching loops.
2. Ground Plane: Run a single, solid ground pour directly under the switching nodes to prevent EMI radiation.
3. Feedback Protection: Keep the feedback trace away from the inductor and switching nodes to avoid noise coupling.
4. Thermal Via Stitching: Add multiple thermal vias under the LM2596 exposed pad to transfer heat to the bottom copper layer.
tips: Switching loops generate electromagnetic interference. Keeping trace loops short is the absolute key to passing FCC emission tests.
The feedback trace should be tapped at the output capacitor, not directly next to the inductor, to read stable voltage levels.

title: Class-D Audio Amplifier PCB
connections: Audio Input L/R → 1µF Input Coupling Capacitors → TPA3116 inputs
TPA3116 Outputs → LC low-pass filter (10µH Inductors & 680nF Caps) → Speaker Terminals
Decoupling Capacitors placed directly at power pins
code:
Layout & Design Steps:
1. Analog & Digital Ground: Separate analog signal ground tracks from noisy high-current power ground pours, connecting them at a single 'star' ground point.
2. LC Filter Placement: Route the output LC filters symmetrically to balance impedance on speaker lines.
3. Bypass Capacitors: Place bypass caps directly at the VCC pins with short traces to GND.
4. Differential Routing: Route input signal lines as differential pairs to reject ambient noise.
tips: Class-D amplifiers use high-frequency PWM switching to achieve high efficiency, requiring careful output filtering to suppress radio frequency carrier noise.
Use high-quality inductors with high saturation currents for the output low-pass filter to prevent audio distortion.

title: ESP32 Custom Development Board
connections: USB-C VBUS → AMS1117 Input → 3.3V Output
CP2102 TXD/RXD → ESP32 RXD0/TXD0 (via series resistors)
Auto-Reset Transistor Circuit (EN & GPIO0) connected to CP2102 DTR & RTS lines
GPIO0/EN pulled up to 3.3V with 10kΩ resistors
code:
Layout & Design Steps:
1. Antenna Clearance: Place the ESP32 module on the board edge, keeping a complete copper keep-out zone under the PCB antenna.
2. Differential USB Lines: Route USB D+/D- lines with 90Ω differential impedance matching rules.
3. LDO Placement: Place decoupling capacitors directly at the input and output pins of the AMS1117 regulator.
4. Power Routing: Set 3.3V power rails to 0.76mm (30 mils) width to support peak ESP32 transmission currents (500mA).
5. Stitching Vias: Create a grid of stitching vias to link top and bottom ground planes.
tips: Never place copper, ground plane, or signal traces under the ESP32 chip antenna; doing so degrades wireless range.
The ESP32 module requires a clean, stable 3.3V supply. A 10µF tantalum capacitor parallel to a 100nF ceramic capacitor at the VDD pin prevents startup issues.
